name: Rajas Con Jitomate (Chile Strips with Tomatoes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the seeds and veins from the chiles and cut them into strips about 1 1/2 inches long and 1/2 inch wide.
2. If the chiles seem too spicy, soak them in salted water for about 30 minutes.
3. Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat.
4. Add the onions to the pan and fry gently until they are soft, taking care not to brown them.
5. Add the chile strips, tomatoes, and salt to the pan with the onions.
6. Cook uncovered over a fairly high flame until the vegetables are well seasoned and the liquid has evaporated, about 10 minutes.
7. This dish is excellent served as a thick sauce over Torta de Elote. It also pairs well with plain broiled meat, fish, or chicken.
